name: Fried Calamari with Spicy Anchovy Mayonnaise
recipeIngredient: 4 cups light olive oil or vegetable oil
recipeInstructions:
1. In a food processor, combine the mayonnaise, anchovies, lemon juice, parsley, and cayenne. Blend until smooth. Transfer the sauce to a bowl and refrigerate, covered tightly, for up to 2 days, until ready to serve.
2. Cut the squid into 1/3-inch rings. If the tentacles are large, halve or quarter them lengthwise. Refrigerate until ready to use.
3. In a heavy-bottomed straight-sided saucepan about 8 inches in diameter, heat the oil to 360 degrees.
4. In a shallow bowl, combine the flour and graham cracker crumbs. Toss 1/3 of the squid in the flour mixture to coat evenly. Shake the squid in a mesh strainer to shed extra coating. Using tongs or a slotted spoon, gently lower each batch of calamari into the hot oil and fry until golden brown, about 2 minutes. Remove with a slotted spoon and drain on paper towels. Sprinkle with salt. The cooked squid can be kept warm in a low oven while you continue.
5. Check your oil temperature and repeat with remaining calamari. Serve hot with chilled anchovy mayonnaise.
Yield: 2 servings
